results.title: 5 Best AI Note Takers for In-Person Meetings [2026]
results.content:
### Plaud Note’s cons

 Users frequently report frustrating experiences with the company's billing practices. Cancelling subscriptions or avoiding automatic annual upgrades is difficult
 Customer service is often slow to respond and relies heavily on canned replies when hardware or software issues arise
 There can be occasional inconsistencies with the transcription accuracy, particularly when dealing with non-standard accents or fast-paced overlapping dialogue

### 📚 Also read:

5 Best AI Meeting Assistants

## So, which AI note taker should you choose for in-person meetings?

Choose an AI note taker for in-person meetings based on what slows you down after each catch-up session. [...] Then I opened my notes.

"pricing… ask him… send thing… next week??"

A masterpiece of literature. Truly. Kafka couldn't have written something more unsettling.

This is exactly why AI note takers for in-person meetings have been on my mind… because somehow, more people in the room means fewer details make it out alive.

People talk over each other. The room has the acoustics of a packed football stadium. The most important decision of the entire meeting gets dropped casually as someone's already got one foot out the door and their jacket halfway on.

And your notes? Your notes are a crime scene with no witnesses.

So I did what any reasonable person drowning in cryptic bullet points would do: I tested AI note takers built for real-world, in-person conversations. [...] ### Notta’s cons

 For Japanese users or teams, Notta uses conversation data to train its AI models by default, with opt-out available only on the Enterprise plan
 Transcription accuracy takes a noticeable hit if the room gets noisy or people start talking over each other
 The platform can get a bit buggy when trying to process or upload really long audio files

### 📚 Also read:

5 Best Notta Alternatives for AI Meeting Notes

## 5. Plaud Note

Best for: Professionals who want a dedicated, screen-free hardware device that records offline meetings anywhere

 

Software-only platforms require you to manage an app on your phone or laptop during in-person meetings. Plaud Note takes a different approach with a physical, standalone AI voice recorder that effortlessly captures the room.

results.title: Best AI Notetakers for In Person Meeting in 2025 | Jamie
results.content:
Current AI note-taking tools work poorly for in-person meetings. Most apps focus on online calls, require expensive hardware, or force you to take notes manually. These solutions fail in real meeting rooms.
 People need an AI notetaker that works during face-to-face meetings. The tool should create accurate transcripts with speaker identification, generate summaries with clear decisions and action items, protect privacy, record offline and in-person, allow quick searches, and connect to existing workflows. [...] For many teams I work with, the biggest problem with AI notes isn't the technology - it's trust. A visible meeting bot makes people nervous (and honestly, it's kind of awkward), and it's hard to know where audio goes, how long it stays there, and whether it's used to train someone else's model. In finance, healthcare, or legal work, this feels impossible to approve, even if the notes would help. [...] ## Why Are People Actively Searching for the Best AI Notetakers for In-Person Meetings?

### “Most tools are made for online calls or pricey gadgets, not simple in-room meetings”

> “I cant seem to find any good solution. Online all I see is physical hardware devices that cost a fortune and have subscriptions. The problem with most solutions out there like otter,ai/fireflies is that i need to be in a meeting. I just want some app that i can turn on, record meeting, and then turn off and review notes.” — r/Entrepreneur, Nov 2025

I keep seeing this same complaint everywhere. When I was researching AI notetakers, I found this user saying "I just want something simple for a real-life meeting, and nothing fits." And you know what? They're absolutely right.

results.title: Best AI Note Taker for In-Person Meetings 2026 (No Bot Required) - KenzNote
results.content:
Reasonable per-file processing time. A one-hour meeting should return results in a few minutes. Some tools throttle processing speed on lower-tier plans, which becomes frustrating when you need notes before your next meeting.

Privacy and data handling. Your audio file contains sensitive business conversations. Review where recordings are stored, whether they are used for AI training, and how long they are retained.

## Top AI Note Takers for In-Person Meetings

### 1. KenzNote - Best for Upload-Based In-Person Recording [...] Mobile recording capability. If the tool has a mobile app with built-in recording, you can capture and process in a single workflow without transferring files. This matters for field teams and anyone who does not want to manage audio files manually.

Speaker identification accuracy. In-person meetings often involve multiple voices around a table, without the neat separation that individual microphones provide in video calls. The AI needs to be good at distinguishing speakers from mixed audio, not just clean mono tracks.

No calendar access required. In-person meeting tools should not need to see your calendar. You are not scheduling a bot. You are uploading a file. Any requirement to connect your calendar is a privacy trade-off that is completely unnecessary for this use case. [...] Board meetings in a conference room. Client visits at your office. Sales pitches at a prospect's location. Workshops and training sessions. Interviews. One-on-ones that happen face to face. All of these produce valuable conversation that needs to be documented, but none of them have a Zoom link for a bot to join.

This is not a niche problem. A significant portion of professional meetings still happen in person. Hybrid work environments mean that some participants are physically present while others dial in remotely. Field teams, sales reps, consultants, and executives spend substantial parts of their week in rooms rather than in front of cameras.

results.title: ‎Minutes: AI Meeting Note Taker App - App Store
results.content: I have been using the app for about two months now, and it has been incredibly helpful in taking notes and keeping track of everything discussed in meetings. However, when I logged in this morning, I found myself automatically logged out, even though my subscription is still active. To make matters worse, all my meeting minutes I regularly review to recall what happened in meetings—are gone.This is extremely frustrating, as those notes were an essential part of my workflow, and there seems to be no way to retrieve them. Additionally, the app’s subscription system is very confusing. Despite my account showing as active, the app still prompts me to subscribe. Why would a customer be asked to subscribe again if their account is already active?This experience has made me lose trust in the

results.title: The 7 Best AI Note-Taker for In-Person Meetings (Plus Buying Guide!)
results.content:
Fathom is an AI meeting assistant built mainly to record, transcribe, and summarize your online meetings only. So, what is it doing on this list of AI note takers for in-person meetings? 

We found a way to work around the in-person recording restriction by starting a virtual meeting app during a face-to-face meeting. So, we guess it is possible for Fathom  to record in person meetings...they quality just won't be that good.

There are a few issues.

For one, this setup can be awkward, and issues with mic quality, feedback, and echo can impact the quality of transcription and summary. 

But free is free. And Fathom is one of the most comprehensive free AI note takers. [...] Also, Granola doesn’t record audio. It just listens. So, if you’re confused about something, you can’t go back and verify what was actually said in full detail.

Overall, Granola is good at what it does, but in our opinion, it doesn’t do enough. We prefer note takers like Plaud that generate real data like searchable transcripts and audio recordings. We might be crazy, but we also like our note taking AI to take notes for us, and not the other way around. 

Granola Pros

 AI-enhanced notes that combine your own writing with automated summaries
 No meeting bots or plugins required
 Templates and “recipes” make notes consistent and actionable
 Works with common video calls and on desktop

Granola Cons [...] Like other apps on this list, Fathom joins your call, captures everything said, and delivers transcripts and clear summaries. Unlike the others, it offers unlimited recording, transcription, and storage for free. 

So, if you’re looking for an in-person AI note taker that’s free and unlimited, this is it. It’s wonky, but if you’re not super concerned with accuracy and collaborative features, it works ok.  

Fathom Pros

 Unlimited transcription and recordings on the free plan
 Automatic summaries and action items
 Integrates with calendars and can sync notes to CRMs
 Search and highlight tools for past meetings

Fathom Cons
